evidence.description: FIND-094 fix VERIFIED. CRIT-800 passThreshold now reads `cargo test --tests` exits 0 with all 91 tests passing (43 unit + 18 editor_handlers + 22 editor_wire_sprint8 + 8 feed_handlers; the editor_wire_sprint8 count includes the iter-3 additions of `prop_ipc_006_saving_key_set_equality` and `ec_ipc_013_whitespace_only_body_is_not_empty`). Arithmetic: 43 + 18 + 22 + 8 = 91 — internally coherent. The previously self-falsifying clause `no additions or removals expected during contract review` was removed and replaced with an explicit reference to the iter-3 additions, restoring meta-consistency.

`ec_ipc_013_whitespace_only_body_is_not_empty` test (added in iter-3) is present at line 1136 and asserts the byte-level `is_note_empty` semantics on `["
", "  ", "

   
", " 	 
"]` for both compose_state_for_select_past_note and compose_state_for_save_ok. Test is unchanged from iter-3 PASS state.

Coverage map header is intact: 20 REQ-IPC entries (lines 31-50, matching `^///\s+REQ-IPC-0(0[1-9]|1[0-9]|20)`) and 14 EC-IPC entries (lines 52-65, matching `^///\s+EC-IPC-0(0[1-9]|1[0-4])`). Line 35 references prop_ipc_006_saving_key_set_equality and line 64 references ec_ipc_013_whitespace_only_body_is_not_empty — both added in iter-3, both present and named consistently in the test file.

Iter-4 patch is scoped to sprint-8.md:40-43; wire_audit.sh is unchanged. FIND-093 fix from iter-3 (shopt -s globstar nullglob at line 18, recursive `**/*.rs` glob at line 209) remains intact. Three grep audits (PROP-IPC-012/020/021) execute as documented.
print_wire_fixtures emits 14-fixture cover set with assertion arr.len() == 14 (line 1190). Unchanged from iter-3.
Iter-4 patch did not touch wire_fixtures() / prop_ipc_010 / the 14-fixture cover set. CRIT-803 (round-trip cover set) and CRIT-811 (TS suite non-regression) remain at iter-3 PASS state.

Iter-4 patch is contract-only (sprint-8.md:40-43); no source changes. CRIT-804 (no legacy 6-arg form), CRIT-805 (skip_serializing_if allow-list, null-literal preservation), CRIT-806 (BlockTypeDto 9-variant kebab-case enum) all remain at iter-3 PASS state.

Iter-4 amendment is strictly limited to the CRIT-800 passThreshold counts; no scope creep into TS-side reducer, Capture domain, or ui-feed-list-actions Sprint 4 deferrals. The CRIT-807 / CRIT-810 deferred-item prohibitions remain intact and unmodified.

summary: PASS: Iter-4 amendment to sprint-8.md:40-43 successfully resolves FIND-094. The CRIT-800 passThreshold now correctly states 91 tests with the breakdown 43 unit + 18 editor_handlers + 22 editor_wire_sprint8 + 8 feed_handlers (sum = 91, arithmetically coherent), and explicitly attributes the editor_wire_sprint8 increase from 20 to 22 to the iter-3 additions of `prop_ipc_006_saving_key_set_equality` and `ec_ipc_013_whitespace_only_body_is_not_empty`. The previously self-falsifying meta-clause `no additions or removals expected during contract review` was replaced with the explicit iter-3 attribution, eliminating the self-incoherence flagged by FIND-094. Both newly-named tests are present in the test file (lines 314-361 and 1135-1161 respectively) and are referenced from the coverage map (lines 35 and 64). The iter-4 patch is correctly scoped: it touches only the threshold text on sprint-8.md:40-43 and introduces no new contradictions in any other criterion. CRIT-801, 802, 803, 804, 805, 806, 807, 808, 809, 810, 811 are unchanged from their iter-3 PASS state. All five strict-mode dimensions PASS; all 12 criteria evaluated and PASS. The Sprint 8 contract is now ready for the strict-mode convergence checker.
